title: Accessibility audit + guide for card templates
context: Flashcard accessibility is often an afterthought: color-only cues exclude colorblind learners, missing alt-text on images excludes screen-reader users, and poorly structured HTML can break in assistive technologies. The style guide v1 includes baseline accessibility conventions, but a dedicated audit and guide provides deeper coverage aligned with a11y-alttext-commons conventions and ensures card templates are genuinely accessible to all learners.
objective: Audit the existing card templates and style guide for accessibility gaps and produce a comprehensive accessibility guide for card template authors.
acceptanceCriteria: Audits the card-writing style guide v1 and any existing card templates for accessibility gaps (color-only cues, missing alt-text, non-semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, screen-reader compatibility).
Produces a guide covering: alt-text writing best practices for flashcard images, semantic HTML requirements for card fields, color contrast requirements, and keyboard/screen-reader compatibility.
Aligns with a11y-alttext-commons conventions (or equivalent recognized accessibility standard).
Includes concrete examples of compliant and non-compliant card HTML for each rule.
Recommends updates to the card-writing style guide v1 and the code-002 linter to enforce any new rules.
output: A versioned accessibility audit and guide document (docs/accessibility-guide.md) with per-rule examples and style guide update recommendations, CC-BY-4.0.
